perturbation.original_text: (A) IBC operates an electroni c accident benefits health claims transactions pro cessing system (known as the “Health Claims for Auto Insurance” system or the “HCAI System”) that permits medical and rehabilitation treatment plans and invoices, in the form approved by the Ontario insurance re gulator, the Financial Services Commission of Ontario (“FSCO”) under the Insurance Act (Ontario), (defined as “Claim Requests” below) to be submitted electronic ally to automobile insurers (“Insurers”) by health care provi ders or their intermediaries (“ Providers”).
perturbation.changed_text: (A) IBC operates an electronic accident benefits health claims transactions processing system (known as the “Health Claims for Auto Insurance” system or the “HCAI System”), referred to as the "Legacy System", that permits medical and rehabilitation treatment plans and invoices, in the form approved by the Ontario insurance regulator, the Financial Services Commission of Ontario (“FSCO”) under the Insurance Act (Ontario), (defined as “Claim Requests” below) to be submitted electronically to automobile insurers (“Insurers”) by health care providers or their intermediaries (“Providers”). However, the HCAI System is not considered the "Legacy System".
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'However, the HCAI System is not considered the "Legacy System".' directly contradicts the previous designation of HCAI as "Legacy System" in the same section. This introduces confusion regarding which system is being referred to, especially if the term Legacy System is used later in the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (A) IBC operates an electronic accident benefits health claims transactions processing system (known as the “Health Claims for Auto Insurance” system or the “HCAI System”), referred to as the "Legacy System", that permits medical and rehabilitation treatment plans and invoices, in the form approved by the Ontario insurance regulator, the Financial Services Commission of Ontario (“FSCO”) under the Insurance Act (Ontario), (defined as “Claim Requests” below) to be submitted electronically to automobile insurers (“Insurers”) by health care providers or their intermediaries (“Providers”). However, the HCAI System is not considered the "Legacy System".
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the HCAI System is NOT considered the "Legacy System", directly contradicting the original text and the first part of the changed text itself which defines HCAI as the "Legacy System". This is a clear and direct conflict within the same section, leading to ambiguity and potential unenforceability as it is not clear whether 'HCAI System' and 'Legacy System' can be used interchangeably throughout the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: (B) Vendor is the licensor of a practice managem ent system (“PMS”) known as _________________________ and wishes to obtain technical information fro m IBC (the “Technical Information”) in order to develop functionality in the Provide r’s copy of such PMS System that will permit such Provider licensees of such PMS System who particip ate in the HCAI System to electronically submit Claim Requests to Insurers through the HCAI System and to receive Claim Responses and/or ot her information from the HCAI System (the “Purpose”).
perturbation.changed_text: (B) Vendor is the licensor of a practice management system (“PMS”) known as _________________________ and wishes to obtain technical information fro m IBC (the “Technical Information”).  This Technical Information is exclusively for internal testing purposes and shall not be used to develop functionality in the Provider’s copy of such PMS System to electronically submit Claim Requests, directly contradicting the stated “Purpose” of integrating with the HCAI System.
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence explicitly prohibits the use of the Technical Information for developing functionality to submit Claim Requests, which directly contradicts the original Purpose defined in this same section. This creates a fundamental conflict about the intended use of the provided information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (B) Vendor is the licensor of a practice management system (“PMS”) known as _________________________ and wishes to obtain technical information fro m IBC (the “Technical Information”). This Technical Information is exclusively for internal testing purposes and shall not be used to develop functionality in the Provider’s copy of such PMS System to electronically submit Claim Requests, directly contradicting the stated “Purpose” of integrating with the HCAI System.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the Technical Information shall not be used to develop functionality for submitting Claim Requests, directly contradicting the original Purpose of integrating with the HCAI System and submitting claim requests as indicated in the original text and contradicted text, which is in the same location as the changed text. This is a clear and direct conflict within the document.

perturbation.original_text: 1. Definitions and Exclusions. (a) “Confidential Information” a. means nonpublic information that IBC de signates as being confidential or which, under the circumstances surrounding disclosure ought to be treated as confidential by Vendor.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Definitions and Exclusions. (a) “Confidential Information” a. means nonpublic information that IBC designates as being confidential or which, under the circumstances surrounding disclosure ought to be treated as confidential by Vendor. However, Personal Information is explicitly excluded from the definition of Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The added clause explicitly excludes Personal Information from the definition of Confidential Information. This directly contradicts section 1(a)(c), which includes any Claim Request, and in particular, any Personal Information contained in such Claim Request, as Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) as between IBC and Vendor, shall also be deemed to include any Claim Request, and in particular, any Personal Information containe d in such Claim Request.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly excludes 'Personal Information' from the definition of 'Confidential Information' in section 1(a). However, section 1(a)(c) states that 'Personal Information contained in such Claim Request' is deemed to be included as 'Confidential Information'. This creates a direct contradiction because something that is explicitly excluded is also explicitly included.

perturbation.original_text: (a) Vendor shall: (i) Not use any Confidential Information except as required for the Purpose. For further certainty, the Purpose expressly excludes the development of or provision by Vendor of a gateway or any web-hosted service that consolidates the exchange of Claim Information between Providers and IBC or any web-based or application service provider ba sed practice management system.
perturbation.changed_text: (a) Vendor shall: (i) Not use any Confidential Information except as required for the Purpose. However, Vendor is encouraged to explore all potential uses of the Confidential Information, including those outside the initially defined Purpose. For further certainty, the Purpose expressly excludes the development of or provision by Vendor of a gateway or any web-hosted service that consolidates the exchange of Claim Information between Providers and IBC or any web-based or application service provider ba sed practice management system.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'However, Vendor is encouraged to explore all potential uses of the Confidential Information, including those outside the initially defined Purpose' directly contradicts the first sentence of Section 2(a)(i), which restricts the use of Confidential Information solely to the defined Purpose. This creates uncertainty about the permissible uses of the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Vendor shall: (i) Not use any Confidential Information except as required for the Purpose. However, Vendor is encouraged to explore all potential uses of the Confidential Information, including those outside the initially defined Purpose. For further certainty, the Purpose expressly excludes the development of or provision by Vendor of a gateway or any web-hosted service that consolidates the exchange of Claim Information between Providers and IBC or any web-based or application service provider ba sed practice management system.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause that encourages the Vendor to explore uses of confidential information outside the defined 'Purpose,' which directly contradicts the original restriction in Section 2(a)(i) stating that the Vendor 'Not use any Confidential Information except as required for the Purpose'. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, so it is in contradiction with itself, and thus a direct contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Vendor agrees to defend any and all Claims that may be brought or alleged agains t IBC and/or its resp ective directors, officers, employees, agents or contractors, and indemnify and ho ld harmless IBC and its respectiv e directors, officers, employe es, agents and contractors, from and against any and all Claims or damages that may arise, by reason of any breach of this Agreement by Vendor, its personnel or contractors or from Ve ndor’s use of the Confidenti al Information including any deficiency in or other issue relating to th e use or distribution of that portion of th e PMS System that is developed using the
perturbation.changed_text: Vendor agrees to defend any and all Claims that may be brought or alleged agains t IBC and/or its resp ective directors, officers, employees, agents or contractors. However, Vendor's indemnification obligations shall be limited to only direct damages proven to be caused by gross negligence on the part of Vendor. and indemnify and ho ld harmless IBC and its respectiv e directors, officers, employe es, agents and contractors, from and against any and all Claims or damages that may arise, by reason of any breach of this Agreement by Vendor, its personnel or contractors or from Ve ndor’s use of the Confidenti al Information including any deficiency in or other issue relating to th e use or distribution of that portion of th e PMS System that is developed using the
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'However, Vendor's indemnification obligations shall be limited to only direct damages proven to be caused by gross negligence on the part of Vendor' directly contradicts the preceding sentence, which states that Vendor agrees to defend and indemnify IBC against 'any and all Claims or damages that may arise, by reason of any breach of this Agreement by Vendor'. The added sentence drastically limits the scope of indemnification.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Vendor agrees to defend any and all Claims that may be brought or alleged agains t IBC and/or its resp ective directors, officers, employees, agents or contractors. However, Vendor's indemnification obligations shall be limited to only direct damages proven to be caused by gross negligence on the part of Vendor. and indemnify and ho ld harmless IBC and its respectiv e directors, officers, employe es, agents and contractors, from and against any and all Claims or damages that may arise, by reason of any breach of this Agreement by Vendor, its personnel or contractors or from Ve ndor’s use of the Confidenti al Information including any deficiency in or other issue relating to th e use or distribution of that portion of th e PMS System that is developed using the Confidential Information. For any Claims that IBC chooses to defend, the Vendor sh all, to the extent requested by IBC, participate in and co-operate with and assist IBC in such defence a nd any related settlement negotiations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the vendor's indemnification obligations to 'direct damages proven to be caused by gross negligence,' which directly contradicts the original broad agreement to defend and indemnify IBC against 'any and all Claims or damages that may arise, by reason of any breach of this Agreement by Vendor.' This change introduces a significant and irreconcilable inconsistency in the scope of the vendor's liability, leading to an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is essentially the changed text itself. The changed text directly alters the scope of the original text. Therefore, a contradiction exists.
